label: Cinderveil Chamber
description: Cinderveil Chamber serves as a private sleeping room cut from the canteen’s vitrified basalt shell and lined with wreck-metal sheeting to blunt auroral backwash. A narrow cot-frame and a wall-bolted locker sit beneath mesh-baffled light strips that maintain the same bruise-hued gloom as the drinking hall beyond. The threshold is sealed with a heavy gasketed hatch and backed by vox-suppression panels, reducing the bastion’s interference to a low, constant thrum. Condenser vents and ash-filters are set into the upper corners, keeping storm grit from settling into the bedding and floor seams.
